message:
refactor(ffs-mvcc): extract validate_transaction_id and apply to all commit paths

The "txn id 0 or u64::MAX is invalid → DurabilityFailure" check was
inlined in ShardedMvccStore::commit but missing from
ShardedMvccStore::commit_ssi, MvccStore::preflight_fcw, and
MvccStore::commit_with_inplace — those four paths could each accept
a transaction with the sentinel ids and only later trip on a deeper
invariant (or worse, silently land a "txn id 0" record that
collides with the tombstone slot in CommittedTxnRecord storage).

Extract the check into a shared helper:

- crate::validate_transaction_id(txn_id: TxnId) -> Result<(), CommitError>
- pub(crate) so both lib.rs and sharded.rs can use it without
  exposing it on the public surface.
- Returns CommitError::DurabilityFailure with "invalid transaction
  id {n}" so the caller error matches the existing message that
  callers already compare against.

Apply at every commit entry point:

- MvccStore::preflight_fcw — propagate via the existing `?` operator
  since this site doesn't own the Transaction.
- MvccStore::commit_with_inplace — preserves the (CommitError,
  Transaction) error tuple shape (returns the txn back to the
  caller for retry/recovery).
- ShardedMvccStore::commit — replace the inlined four-line check
  with a single call; behavior is identical, including returning
  the txn back via the error tuple.
- ShardedMvccStore::commit_ssi — was previously missing the check
  entirely. Add at the top of the function before the empty-write-set
  fast path so SSI never even tries to schedule a sentinel txn id.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
